  have hNotPicky : ¬PickyEater Hannah := R4 Hannah (And.intro hAt hEnjoy)
  have hSpend : SpendsTimeInDiningHalls Hannah := R5 Hannah (And.intro hAt hEnjoy)
  exact And.intro hAt (And.intro hNotPicky hSpend)

axiom LiveAtHome : obj → Prop
axiom LiveWithStrangers : obj → Prop

-- Axioms from the context
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, MB x → SpendTimeWithSibs x
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, SibsBornTogether x → MB x
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, ComplainsOften x → SibsBornTogether x
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, LiveAtHome x → ¬ LiveWithStrangers x
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, SpendTimeWithSibs x → LiveAtHome x
axiom A6 : (MB Luke ∧ LiveWithStrangers Luke) ∨ (¬ MB Luke ∧ ¬ LiveWithStrangers Luke)

theorem luke_neither_mb_nor_complains : ¬ MB Luke ∧ ¬ ComplainsOften Luke := by
  -- From MB ⇒ SpendTime ⇒ LiveAtHome ⇒ ¬ LiveWithStrangers
  have h_not_lws_if_mb : ∀ x : obj, MB x → ¬ LiveWithStrangers x :=
    fun x hmb => A4 x (A5 x (A1 x hmb))
  -- The left disjunct in A6 is impossible
  have h_contra_left : (MB Luke ∧ LiveWithStrangers Luke) → False :=
    fun h => (h_not_lws_if_mb Luke h.left) h.right
  -- Hence the right disjunct must hold
  have h_right : (¬ MB Luke ∧ ¬ LiveWithStrangers Luke) :=
    Or.resolve_left A6 h_contra_left
  have h_not_mb : ¬ MB Luke := h_right.left
  -- From Complains ⇒ SibsBornTogether ⇒ MB, so with ¬MB we get ¬Complains
  have h_mb_of_complains : ComplainsOften Luke → MB Luke :=
    fun hc => A2 Luke (A3 Luke hc)
  have h_not_complains : ¬ ComplainsOften Luke :=
    fun hc => h_not_mb (h_mb_of_complains hc)
  exact And.intro h_not_mb h_not_complains

-- If people complain often about annoying siblings, then they have siblings who were born together

-- If people spend a lot of time with siblings, then they live at home

      have hNotComplains : ¬ Complains Luke := by

        have hMult : Multiple Luke := R2 Luke hSibsTogether
        exact hNotMult hMult

-- If there is a drought, there is a watering ban.

-- Either Mark plants a garden or there is a drought.
axiom H3 : PlantsGarden Mark ∨ Drought
-- If there is a watering ban, then for any person, their plants won't get enough water.
axiom H4 : WateringBan → ∀ p : Person, NotEnoughWater p
-- If the plants don't get watered enough, the garden will fail to thrive.
axiom H5 : ∀ p : Person, NotEnoughWater p → GardenFail p

  have pg_to_nc : PlantsGarden Mark → NeedCare Mark :=

  have not_pg : ¬ PlantsGarden Mark :=
    fun pg => NC (pg_to_nc pg)
  have d : Drought := by
    cases H3 with
    | inl pg => exact False.elim (not_pg pg)
    | inr d => exact d
  have wb : WateringBan := H2 d
  have new : NotEnoughWater Mark := (H4 wb) Mark
  exact H5 Mark new

axiom W : Prop   -- weekend coming up
axiom R : Prop   -- relax
axiom F : Prop   -- finish tasks
axiom C : Prop   -- clear mind
axiom O : Prop   -- organized
axiom T : Prop   -- made a to-do list

axiom P1 : W → R               -- If weekend, then relax
axiom P2 : F → C               -- If finish tasks, then clear mind
axiom P3 : W ∨ ¬ C             -- Either weekend or not clear mind
axiom P4 : O → F               -- If organized, then finish tasks
axiom P5 : T → O               -- If to-do list, then organized
axiom notR : ¬ R               -- Alex does not relax

theorem no_to_do_list : ¬ T := by
  intro hT                     -- Assume T (made to-do list)
  have hO : O := P5 hT         -- T → O
  have hF : F := P4 hO         -- O → F
  have hC : C := P2 hF         -- F → C
  have h_notW : ¬ W := fun hW => notR (P1 hW)  -- ¬R and W → R imply ¬W
  cases P3 with                -- Case split on W ∨ ¬C
  | inl hW => exact h_notW hW  -- W case: contradicts ¬W
  | inr h_negC => exact h_negC hC  -- ¬C case: contradicts C

If someone finishes their tasks, then they have a clear mind. Either the weekend was coming, or they did not have a clear mind. If someone is organized, then they finish their tasks. If they made a to-do list, then they are organized.
conclusion: Alex does not relax this weekend, did he make a to-do list?

axiom P5 : ∀ (p : Person), MadeTodoList p → Organized p

-- Given: Alex does not relax

  -- Assume Alex made a to-do list for contradiction

  -- Get task completion from P4

  -- Get clear mind from P3

    intro w

  cases P2 with
  | inl w => contradiction
  | inr no_clear => 
      -- h_clear proves ClearMind Alex, contradicting no_clear
      exact no_clear h_clear
